skip_reasons.reason: The unattended runner cannot respond to operating-system camera permission prompts or provide stable physical-camera input. The test therefore avoids opening a real session on targets where doing so could hang CI or produce nondeterministic frames.
skip_reasons.platform_support: A skipped camera test does not mean the Codename One port is unsupported. Every listed target remains a supported port; camera availability is a separate runtime capability and depends on the device, permissions, and camera backend.
skip_reasons.verification: The native camera implementations are compiled in their port builds and exercised with granted permissions on real hardware or an interactive browser. Deterministic API assertions use the synthetic camera backend outside this portability table.

skip_reasons.reason: The JavaScript test depends on browser accessibility semantics that are not exposed through the headless worker bridge used by this runner.
skip_reasons.platform_support: This is a declared JavaScript test limitation. It is not counted as a pass and does not describe accessibility behavior on the native ports.
skip_reasons.verification: Native targets run the automated accessibility contract. Browser accessibility requires a separate interactive browser and assistive-technology check until the worker bridge exposes the required semantics.

skip_reasons.reason: The JavaScript runtime uses a browser worker and does not implement the native background-thread UI access path exercised by this test.
skip_reasons.platform_support: The skip is limited to this JavaScript-specific runtime contract. Native targets continue to run the same assertion.
skip_reasons.verification: The test runs on the native ports. JavaScript thread and UI scheduling are covered by browser-worker lifecycle tests until this native-style path is implemented.

skip_reasons.reason: This test measures a native Base64 bridge. The JavaScript port uses its browser implementation and has no equivalent native bridge to invoke.
skip_reasons.platform_support: Base64 remains available on the web port; only the native-bridge assertion and timing path are inapplicable there.
skip_reasons.verification: Native targets run this bridge test. The JavaScript implementation is covered by the shared Base64 correctness tests and the common workload benchmark.

skip_reasons.reason: The iframe load callback is not currently routed from the browser host into the worker, so the unattended test cannot reach its deterministic capture point.
skip_reasons.platform_support: BrowserComponent support is not inferred from this skip. The missing worker callback remains an explicit JavaScript-port gap.
skip_reasons.verification: Native ports run the screenshot test. The web path is checked interactively while the worker callback transport is being completed.

skip_reasons.reason: This translator regression exercises native generated-code behavior that is not applicable to the JavaScript bytecode runtime.
skip_reasons.platform_support: The JavaScript port uses a different translation and execution pipeline, so this native-code assertion is not evidence for or against general web support.
skip_reasons.verification: Native translator jobs run the regression. JavaScript translation is covered by its own compiler and browser-stage harnesses.

skip_reasons.reason: Browser workers do not expose a phone-call state API equivalent to the native mobile integration exercised by this test.
skip_reasons.platform_support: Call detection is unavailable in the JavaScript port. The rest of the web port remains covered independently.
skip_reasons.verification: Supported mobile targets run the API contract with their native implementation; device-level behavior is verified on telephony-capable hardware.

skip_reasons.reason: The combined XY chart enters a non-terminating layout path in the JavaScript runtime before the per-test capture deadline can start.
skip_reasons.platform_support: This chart variant is a known JavaScript runtime gap, not an intermittent pass. Other chart tests remain active on the web port.
skip_reasons.verification: The complete chart test runs on other ports. JavaScript keeps the remaining chart matrix active while this layout defect is tracked separately.

skip_reasons.reason: The JavaScript implementation does not yet provide the AES, RSA, signature, and secure-random bridge required by this test.
skip_reasons.platform_support: Cryptographic support is not claimed from a skipped result. This is an explicit JavaScript-port capability gap.
skip_reasons.verification: Native ports run the shared crypto assertions. The web row remains skipped until a browser crypto bridge passes the same contract.

skip_reasons.reason: The missing-file read can stall in the JavaScript LocalForage host call and degrade the worker channel for every test that follows.
skip_reasons.platform_support: This is a known JavaScript storage-bridge defect. It is skipped deterministically instead of being allowed to fail or hang intermittently.
skip_reasons.verification: Other storage tests stay active on JavaScript, and native ports run this exact missing-file contract. The skip is removed when the LocalForage call completes reliably.

skip_reasons.reason: The JavaScript runtime currently formats some floating-point boundary values differently from the shared Java string contract.
skip_reasons.platform_support: The mismatch is a declared JavaScript compatibility gap. It is not treated as a flaky test or a successful result.
skip_reasons.verification: Native ports run the shared formatting assertions. JavaScript remains skipped until every boundary case produces the required Java representation.

skip_reasons.reason: The textured model load wedges the headless SwiftShader WebGL path, and its rasterization is not stable across hosted runner architectures.
skip_reasons.platform_support: This skip is limited to the headless JavaScript CI environment. Simpler WebGL geometry, texture, and animation tests continue to run there.
skip_reasons.verification: The model test runs on real-GPU targets. JavaScript keeps cube, textured-cube, and animation coverage active in the deterministic suite.

skip_reasons.reason: The animated lightweight picker popup can starve the JavaScript worker paint needed to complete the screenshot, leaving the test deadlocked.
skip_reasons.platform_support: The skip records a specific JavaScript popup-lifecycle defect. Other picker and validation tests remain active.
skip_reasons.verification: Native ports run the screenshot. The web test returns only after popup editing and capture complete deterministically.

skip_reasons.reason: Browser workers do not expose the native local-notification override hook exercised by this test.
skip_reasons.platform_support: This native integration is unavailable on the JavaScript port; the skip is not a general web-port failure.
skip_reasons.verification: Native targets run the override contract. Browser notification behavior requires its separate service-worker and permission environment.

skip_reasons.reason: The large stack-allocation pattern corrupts JavaScript runtime state and can prevent every later canvas test from running.
skip_reasons.platform_support: This remains an explicit JavaScript runtime defect. The test is parked before execution so it cannot create intermittent downstream failures.
skip_reasons.verification: Native backends run the SIMD assertion. JavaScript can re-enable it only after the translated allocation path passes without changing shared runtime state.

skip_reasons.reason: The JavaScript runtime does not yet match Java's regular-expression replacement behavior for a greedy quantified pattern.
skip_reasons.platform_support: This is a declared JavaScript compatibility gap, not evidence that the shared String API passed.
skip_reasons.verification: Native ports run the full String contract. JavaScript remains skipped until the same input and replacement produce the Java result.

skip_reasons.reason: The JavaScript runtime does not currently parse the fixed-offset GMT-05:00 identifier required by the shared time-zone contract.
skip_reasons.platform_support: The skip records a specific JavaScript time-zone compatibility gap. Other time APIs continue to run independently.
skip_reasons.verification: Native ports run the complete time contract. JavaScript returns to the matrix when fixed-offset identifiers match Java behavior.

skip_reasons.reason: Browsers do not expose the native network-interface state needed to determine VPN usage from a worker.
skip_reasons.platform_support: VPN detection is unavailable on the JavaScript port because of the browser security boundary. This does not affect unrelated networking support.
skip_reasons.verification: Native targets run the API assertion against their platform integration. The browser result stays skipped unless a reliable browser capability becomes available.

skip_reasons.reason: This assertion requires a working video encoder as well as a decoder. Apple simulators, constrained-device runners, and the JavaScript CI runtime do not expose a stable encoder to the headless job, so the encode/decode round trip is skipped there.
skip_reasons.platform_support: The port remains supported. Video playback and frame decoding are measured separately by VideoIODecodedFramesScreenshotTest; this skip is limited to creating a new encoded video in that CI environment.
skip_reasons.verification: Encoder-backed targets run the full counting-frame and audio round trip. Apple media playback and decoding are covered separately, while device-only recording is verified in signed hardware builds.

features.name: Real camera and media capture
features.description: Live preview, still capture, and device camera selection through the native camera backend.
features.testing: Build each native backend, then run permission-granted capture checks on hardware or an interactive browser and verify a real frame and saved image.
features.why_not_automated: A headless portability run cannot accept consent dialogs, guarantee a camera device, or compare nondeterministic sensor frames.

coverage.label: CameraX
coverage.detail: Native CameraX backend; runtime permission and hardware required.

coverage.label: AVFoundation
coverage.detail: Native AVFoundation backend; signed build, entitlement, permission, and camera hardware required.

coverage.label: MediaDevices
coverage.detail: Browser mediaDevices backend when the page is secure and the user grants access.

coverage.label: Native camera
coverage.detail: Native desktop camera backend when a compatible host camera is available.

coverage.label: Media Foundation
coverage.detail: Native Media Foundation camera backend when the host exposes a camera.

coverage.label: No camera
coverage.detail: The target form factor does not expose a Codename One camera-capture backend.

features.name: Microphone and live audio recording
features.description: Audio capture from a physical microphone through the platform media stack.
features.testing: Use a signed permission-granted build, record a calibrated tone or voice sample, and verify duration, level, and playback on the target device.
features.why_not_automated: Hosted runners do not provide consistent microphones and operating systems deliberately require interactive consent.

coverage.label: Native recorder
coverage.detail: Native media recorder with runtime permission and physical input.

coverage.label: Browser capture
coverage.detail: Available through browser media capture on a secure origin after consent.

coverage.label: Host audio
coverage.detail: Available when the native desktop media stack exposes an input device.

coverage.label: No suite backend
coverage.detail: No general-purpose Codename One recording backend is declared for this target.

features.name: Live AR and physical motion sensors
features.description: AR sessions plus accelerometer, gyroscope, and other readings from real hardware.
features.testing: Run the AR or sensor sample on supported hardware, verify session startup and sensor updates, and compare motion against a controlled physical action.
features.why_not_automated: Simulators do not provide a real camera pose, AR runtime, or reproducible physical movement; the automated row checks API contracts and unsupported fallbacks instead.

coverage.label: ARCore / sensors
coverage.detail: Available on compatible devices with the required vendor services and sensors.

coverage.label: ARKit / sensors
coverage.detail: Available on compatible iPhone or iPad hardware.

coverage.label: Motion sensors
coverage.detail: Motion sensing is form-factor dependent; general AR sessions are not applicable.

coverage.label: Browser sensors
coverage.detail: Browser sensor APIs depend on browser policy, secure context, consent, and hardware.

coverage.label: Fallback contract
coverage.detail: The portable API reports that no live AR or motion backend is present.

features.name: Location, background location, and geofencing
features.description: Foreground fixes, monitored regions, and background location delivery.
features.testing: Install a signed build, grant the appropriate permission level, feed known routes or physically cross a geofence, and verify foreground and background callbacks.
features.why_not_automated: The result depends on user permission, GPS/radio state, operating-system scheduling, and physical movement outside the hosted runner.

coverage.label: Native location
coverage.detail: Native foreground and background location subject to OS permissions and policy.

coverage.label: Apple location
coverage.detail: Available where the target and entitlement expose Core Location behavior.

coverage.label: Geolocation
coverage.detail: Foreground browser geolocation on a secure origin after consent; background behavior is browser-limited.

coverage.label: No declared backend
coverage.detail: No complete location and geofencing backend is declared for this portability target.

features.name: Remote push notifications
features.description: Registration, token delivery, background receipt, actions, and badges through platform push services.
features.testing: Use a signed app and real provider credentials, register a device, send from the push service, and verify foreground, background, and launch delivery.
features.why_not_automated: Push requires certificates or provider keys, an externally reachable service, a uniquely registered installation, and asynchronous OS delivery.

coverage.label: FCM
coverage.detail: Firebase Cloud Messaging with app credentials and notification permission.

coverage.label: APNs
coverage.detail: Apple Push Notification service with signing entitlements and permission.

coverage.label: Web Push
coverage.detail: Supported by compatible browsers on HTTPS with service-worker and provider setup.

coverage.label: Apple entitlement
coverage.detail: Depends on the signed macOS target and its notification entitlements.

coverage.label: No standalone backend
coverage.detail: No current standalone Codename One remote-push backend is declared for this target.

features.name: In-app purchases and subscriptions
features.description: Store product lookup, checkout, receipt restore, subscription state, and management UI.
features.testing: Create sandbox products, sign with a store account, complete purchase and restore flows, and validate receipts against the store or Commerce backend.
features.why_not_automated: The flow requires store-side product configuration, signed identities, sandbox accounts, payment UI, and mutable server receipt state.

coverage.label: Play Billing
coverage.detail: Google Play purchase and subscription flow in a store-installed build.

coverage.label: StoreKit
coverage.detail: Apple StoreKit purchase, restore, and subscription flow.

coverage.label: StoreKit
coverage.detail: Requires a signed Mac App Store configuration and matching products.

coverage.label: No store adapter
coverage.detail: No standalone Codename One store-purchase adapter is declared for this target.

features.name: Contacts and calendar integration
features.description: Read or select contacts and create platform calendar entries through protected personal-data APIs.
features.testing: Populate a device test account, grant access, exercise read/select/write operations, and verify the result in the native contacts or calendar application.
features.why_not_automated: Access is permission-gated and the expected result lives in private user databases and native UI outside the test process.

coverage.label: Native stores
coverage.detail: Native contacts and calendar services after the user grants access.

coverage.label: Apple services
coverage.detail: Availability depends on the macOS target, entitlement, and user account.

coverage.label: No portable store
coverage.detail: The target does not expose a complete Codename One personal-data backend.

features.name: Biometric authentication and protected secrets
features.description: Fingerprint, face, or iris authentication bound to platform keystore material.
features.testing: Enroll biometrics on hardware, run success, cancellation, lockout, and enrollment-change cases, and verify protected-secret invalidation.
features.why_not_automated: Hosted runners have no enrolled biometric hardware and native prompts are intentionally controlled by the user and secure hardware.

coverage.label: BiometricPrompt
coverage.detail: Android BiometricPrompt or legacy fingerprint support, backed by Android Keystore.

coverage.label: LocalAuthentication
coverage.detail: Face ID or Touch ID through LocalAuthentication and Keychain.

coverage.label: NOT_AVAILABLE
coverage.detail: The portable API returns its documented non-supporting fallback.

features.name: NFC tags, smart cards, and host card emulation
features.description: NDEF read/write, tag technology exchange, and platform-dependent card emulation.
features.testing: Present known physical tags and cards, verify payloads and errors, and use a certified reader for host-card-emulation exchanges.
features.why_not_automated: NFC requires short-range hardware, physical tags or readers, user presentation, and platform entitlements that hosted runners lack.

coverage.label: Android NFC
coverage.detail: NDEF, tag technologies, and HCE on compatible hardware.

coverage.label: Core NFC
coverage.detail: NDEF and selected tag technologies; HCE is restricted by iOS version and region.

coverage.label: NOT_AVAILABLE
coverage.detail: The API returns its documented non-supporting fallback.

features.name: Bluetooth Low Energy
features.description: Peripheral discovery, connection, services, characteristics, notifications, and writes through the maintained Bluetooth library.
features.testing: Use a known peripheral or protocol simulator, scan and connect, then verify characteristic reads, writes, notifications, reconnects, and permission denial.
features.why_not_automated: Radio state, nearby peripherals, pairing, permissions, and timing are external to the deterministic screenshot runner.

coverage.label: cn1-bluetooth
coverage.detail: Maintained native library backend on supported phone and tablet hardware.

coverage.label: Host-dependent
coverage.detail: Desktop hardware support depends on the selected maintained library backend and host adapter.

coverage.label: No declared backend
coverage.detail: No maintained general-purpose Bluetooth backend is declared for this target.

features.name: Background fetch and long-running device work
features.description: Operating-system scheduled callbacks and policy-compliant work while the application is not foregrounded.
features.testing: Install a signed build, configure the required modes, background or terminate it, and observe callbacks across OS throttling and restart scenarios.
features.why_not_automated: Scheduling is deliberately nondeterministic, power-policy controlled, entitlement dependent, and often takes longer than a CI job window.

coverage.label: Native scheduler
coverage.detail: Platform background mechanisms subject to OS quotas, permissions, and lifecycle policy.

coverage.label: Target-dependent
coverage.detail: Availability depends on the macOS application mode and signed capabilities.

coverage.label: No equivalent contract
coverage.detail: No equivalent Codename One background-fetch contract is declared for this target.

features.name: Root, jailbreak, and hardware-backed attestation
features.description: Runtime compromise signals plus signed Play Integrity or App Attest tokens.
features.testing: Use release-like signed builds, compromised and clean devices, fresh server nonces, and backend verification of vendor-signed verdicts.
features.why_not_automated: Trust verdicts depend on hardware-backed keys, store-distributed builds, vendor services, device state, and an application backend.

coverage.label: Play Integrity
coverage.detail: Root signals and optional Google Play Integrity when enabled and verified by a backend.

coverage.label: App Attest
coverage.detail: Jailbreak signals and optional Apple App Attest when enabled and verified by a backend.

coverage.label: Unsupported fallback
coverage.detail: No hardware-backed Codename One attestation backend is declared for this target.

features.name: Document, gallery, and native file pickers
features.description: User-mediated selection of media and documents from platform-owned providers.
features.testing: Seed platform storage, launch the native picker, select and cancel items, and verify temporary URI or security-scoped access after returning to the app.
features.why_not_automated: The decisive UI and permissions belong to another process, and hosted runners do not share an identical populated media or document library.

coverage.label: Native picker
coverage.detail: Uses the target's file, document, or browser picker; available types and access rules vary by OS.

coverage.label: No general picker
coverage.detail: The form factor has no general-purpose Codename One document/gallery picker.

features.name: Share sheets, email, SMS, dialing, and external URLs
features.description: Hand-off to platform applications and user-selected communication targets.
features.testing: Open each native chooser or handler on a configured device, complete and cancel actions, and verify returned result metadata where the OS supplies it.
features.why_not_automated: Installed applications, accounts, SIM capability, chooser UI, and user selection are outside the app process and differ per runner.

coverage.label: Native intents
coverage.detail: Native share and communication handlers when the device has a matching service.

coverage.label: OS/browser handler
coverage.detail: Uses an installed desktop handler, browser capability, or portable fallback where available.

coverage.label: Form-factor limited
coverage.detail: No general-purpose Codename One communication chooser is declared for this target.

features.name: Native in-app review prompt
features.description: Store-owned rating prompt with a portable Codename One fallback sheet.
features.testing: Use a store-eligible build and account, request the prompt under vendor quota rules, and verify fallback behavior separately.
features.why_not_automated: Apple and Google intentionally decide whether the native prompt appears, so a request cannot deterministically assert visible store UI.

coverage.label: Play Review
coverage.detail: Google Play In-App Review when the build includes the service.

coverage.label: StoreKit
coverage.detail: StoreKit review request subject to Apple's display quota.

coverage.label: Portable fallback
coverage.detail: Codename One shows its built-in rating sheet when no native prompt is available.

features.name: Live native and web map providers
features.description: Provider tiles, credentials, native peers, gestures, and network-delivered map content.
features.testing: Use valid provider keys on a networked device, load known coordinates, exercise gestures and markers, and confirm provider attribution and fallback behavior.
features.why_not_automated: Provider keys are secrets, map imagery changes independently, usage may be billed, and native peers or network tiles are not deterministic pixels.

coverage.label: Native provider
coverage.detail: Native provider when configured, with the portable map renderer as fallback.

coverage.label: Web provider
coverage.detail: Browser map provider with API key and network access, plus portable fallback.

coverage.label: Portable map
coverage.detail: Portable vector or tile rendering is used when no native provider is active.

features.name: Live advertising networks
features.description: Real provider SDK initialization, inventory requests, consent, click-through, and revenue callbacks.
features.testing: Use provider test-unit identifiers in a signed build, complete consent flows, and verify fill, impression, click, and lifecycle callbacks.
features.why_not_automated: Ad inventory, consent state, provider accounts, network policy, and SDK UI are external and nondeterministic; the first table uses deterministic mock ads.

coverage.label: Provider SDK
coverage.detail: Supported through configured native ad libraries and provider test units.

coverage.label: Web integration
coverage.detail: Depends on the selected web advertising integration and hosting policy.

coverage.label: Mock/custom
coverage.detail: The portable ad component can be tested with mock or application-provided content; no bundled live network is claimed.

features.name: Widgets, Live Activities, and OS-owned surfaces
features.description: The final operating-system presentation of shared surface documents outside the main app window.
features.testing: Install the correctly signed app or extension, publish timelines and actions, then inspect the launcher, lock screen, Dynamic Island, notification area, or desktop host.
features.why_not_automated: The first table checks serialization, rasterization, timelines, and dispatch, but the final surface is rendered by a separate OS process with signing and entitlement requirements.

coverage.label: Widgets/notifications
coverage.detail: Lowers supported surfaces to Android widgets and ongoing notifications.

coverage.label: WidgetKit/ActivityKit
coverage.detail: Requires supported OS versions, extension packaging, and entitlements.

coverage.label: Desktop surface
coverage.detail: Uses the desktop floating-window or preview presentation available to the port.

coverage.label: Windows surfaces
coverage.detail: Floating widgets are supported; Widgets Board integration requires MSIX and Windows App SDK packaging.

coverage.label: In-app preview
coverage.detail: Portable document and rasterizer behavior is available without an OS widget host.

coverage.label: No declared lowering
coverage.detail: No standalone Codename One external-surface lowering is declared for this target.

features.name: Real screen-reader and assistive-technology behavior
features.description: How native accessibility services announce, focus, activate, and navigate the rendered application.
features.testing: Enable the platform screen reader, traverse representative forms, verify announcements and actions, and repeat with dynamic content and input devices.
features.why_not_automated: The first table validates semantics and API state, while the final speech, focus order, gestures, and switch-control behavior belong to external OS services.

coverage.label: OS assistive tech
coverage.detail: Semantic output is consumed by the screen reader or accessibility stack available on that OS.

coverage.label: Form-factor service
coverage.detail: Behavior depends on the target's accessibility service and navigation model.

features.name: Signing, packaging, entitlements, and native SDK integration
features.description: Production artifacts, platform metadata, native interfaces, third-party SDKs, and architecture-specific binaries.
features.testing: Compile each architecture, inspect the generated package, sign with platform credentials, install it, and exercise the integrated native entry point or SDK in its target environment.
features.why_not_automated: These checks happen before app startup or require private signing identities, store portals, proprietary SDK credentials, and platform-specific source rather than one shared runtime assertion.

coverage.label: Target build
coverage.detail: The port has its own build, packaging, and native-integration path; exact signing and extension capabilities are target specific.
